assessment.summary_for_seller: Workable run, but trust and next step are not landing on all key points yet.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message is too generic and misses the signal context.
criteria.evidence_quote: Olga, I know you just went through a panic cycle after the bank communication hit your contractors. I am not going to pitch over that. What matters most to get back right now: predictability for your contractors or a clear incident path for your team?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.
criteria.evidence_quote: Because the incident you just had did not come from the payment tool — it came from the communication layer. When compliance moves and contractors are the last to know, that is an ops process gap. Mellow handles the incident path: who gets notified, in what order, with what documentation. Wise does not do that.
criteria.label: Scope & responsibility boundary

criteria.name: Language fit
criteria.short_reason: Tone and delivery are not adapted to the chosen persona.
criteria.evidence_quote: Because the incident you just had did not come from the payment tool — it came from the communication layer. When compliance moves and contractors are the last to know, that is an ops process gap. Mellow handles the incident path: who gets notified, in what order, with what documentation. Wise does not do that.
criteria.label: Persona language fit

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: Because the incident you just had did not come from the payment tool — it came from the communication layer. When compliance moves and contractors are the last to know, that is an ops process gap. Mellow handles the incident path: who gets notified, in what order, with what documentation. Wise does not do that.

objections.objection: Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?
And on cost: what does this actually run, and is there a trial or pilot option?
Send this in writing and keep it short: who informs people, what the incident path looks like, and what they know in advance. If that is clear, we can continue.

criteria.label: Core objection handling

criteria.name: Next step
criteria.evidence_quote: I will send you a short description: what Mellow controls in an incident, how the notification flow works, and the SLA. A 15-minute review call this week — does that work for you?
criteria.label: Clear next step

transcript.text: Olga, I know you just went through a panic cycle after the bank communication hit your contractors. I am not going to pitch over that. What matters most to get back right now: predictability for your contractors or a clear incident path for your team?
Ok but crypto + Wise has worked for two years. Why do I need this specifically now?

Because the incident you just had did not come from the payment tool — it came from the communication layer. When compliance moves and contractors are the last to know, that is an ops process gap. Mellow handles the incident path: who gets notified, in what order, with what documentation. Wise does not do that.
And on cost: what does this actually run, and is there a trial or pilot option?
I will send you a short description: what Mellow controls in an incident, how the notification flow works, and the SLA. A 15-minute review call this week — does that work for you?
Send this in writing and keep it short: who informs people, what the incident path looks like, and what they know in advance. If that is clear, we can continue.
